snippets.title: Madhu Guru on ai
snippets.takeaway: The biggest challenge for trad PMs trying to adapt to AI native building is a lack of magical thinking.
snippets.summary: Madhu Guru (realmadhuguru on X) shared 1 recent update. The biggest challenge for trad PMs trying to adapt to AI native building is a lack of magical thinking. A decade of frameworks, “agile” and metric obsession has led to constraint-first, incremental thinking. I used to combat this on my teams by asking them to imagine we had tech from 100 yrs in the future, the experiences it would enable and working backward from that. the ideas would always be far more creative.

snippets.title: Amjad Masad on models and product
snippets.takeaway: AI is expensive to run partly because most workloads today run on generic hardware designed pre-LLMs. Etched is the first system designed from the ground up for modern inference.

snippets.title: Guillermo Rauch on agents and enterprise
snippets.takeaway: Vercel Services You can now collocate e.g.: a Python backend API, an ExpressJS server, and a React SPA in one Vercel project.
snippets.summary: Guillermo Rauch (rauchg on X) shared 3 recent updates. Vercel Services You can now collocate e.g.: a Python backend API, an ExpressJS server, and a React SPA in one Vercel project. tl:dr; ▪️ You can run all locally with 𝚟𝚌 𝚍𝚎𝚟 ▪️ Deploy and rollback all at once ▪️ Observe, monitor, debug together ▪️ Internal networking At dinner, tech executive is relaying his company’s @vercel feedback, and then his 12-year-old son’s @vercel feedback 😁 Vercel is for everyone.

snippets.title: Aaron Levie on agents and coding
snippets.takeaway: More data is showing the opposite of what many people expected with AI adoption and jobs. Ramp found that the more AI adoption a company has the more their headcount grows.
snippets.summary: Aaron Levie (levie on X) shared 3 recent updates. More data is showing the opposite of what many people expected with AI adoption and jobs. Ramp found that the more AI adoption a company has the more their headcount grows. At Box, we recently did a survey of 1,600+ mid and large sized companies, and the findings were similar. 58% of respondents expected headcount to rise over the next three years.

snippets.title: Claude on agents and enterprise
snippets.takeaway: Sonnet 5 is a substantial improvement over Sonnet 4.6 on reasoning, tool use, coding, and knowledge work. Its performance is close to Opus 4.8, at lower prices.
snippets.summary: Claude (claudeai on X) shared 3 recent updates. Sonnet 5 is a substantial improvement over Sonnet 4.6 on reasoning, tool use, coding, and knowledge work. Its performance is close to Opus 4.8, at lower prices. Early access partners found Sonnet 5 finishes complex tasks where previous Sonnets stopped short, checks its own output without being asked, and does all its agentic work at an attractive price point.

snippets.title: Why Hardware-Software Co-Design Is AI's Real 100x: Dylan Patel of SemiAnalysis
snippets.takeaway: Why Hardware-Software Co-Design Is AI's Real 100x: Dylan Patel of SemiAnalysis. Like demands for long rise agents skyrocketing supply, all these data center build outs are delayed.
snippets.summary: Like demands for long rise agents skyrocketing supply, all these data center build outs are delayed. Mean, obviously use of tokens is going be the biggest market, and the value that's created from tokens is going be the biggest market. Curve of productivity that we're going to get, and so inference deployments is going be huge.
